fresh_facts.claim: President Trump ordered US forces to 'shoot and kill' any Iranian boats laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz and enforce a naval blockade with recent tanker seizures.
A Pentagon video released on April 24, 2026 shows US forces seizing a second Iranian tanker amid the US-Iran war entering its ninth week.
US Central Command has turned around over 30 ships in the Hormuz blockade with thousands more US forces heading to the region.
Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps fired on a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz, followed by a separate cargo ship attack.
Iranian state media released video of masked commandos seizing two ships on Wednesday as retaliation for US actions.
About half of Iran's ballistic missile stockpile remains intact post-ceasefire.
Iran's economy has lost over 10% of annual GDP ($5 billion) from the blockade.
Iran's Supreme Leader issued a rare public statement amid mounting war and internal pressure, signaling resolve but raising leadership stability questions.
Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf stepped down as head of the negotiating team amid disputes with hardliners.
A secret letter to Mojtaba Khamenei triggered a 'unity' campaign after Trump's comments on leadership disarray, highlighting hardliner influence.
ISW reports rifts between Ghalibaf pushing for diplomacy and IRGC Commander Ahmad Vahidi opposed to US talks.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Israeli media noted on April 19 inability to contact Mojtaba Khamenei as an obstacle to negotiations.
A Provisional Leadership Council comprising Pezeshkian, Mohseni-Ejei, and Arafi is in place following the leadership crisis post-Khamenei.
The Assembly of Experts strike in Qom on March 3 disrupted succession planning.
Ghalibaf's resignation coincides with upcoming Islamabad talks where Araghchi plans a visit with US figures Witkoff and Kushner attending.
The ceasefire expires April 22 and is viewed as the last diplomatic chance before escalation.
Israeli PM coordinates with the US for pressure on Iran and Lebanon peace.
The IRGC fired on a Hormuz tanker on April 18, reimposing blockade after a reversal, risking ceasefire breach.
ISW tracks severe unrest in Iran since January 2026 suppressed with internet blackouts, Starlink confiscations, and orders to 'crush by any means.'
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War / New York Times

fresh_facts.claim: Dozens of security forces were arrested in Kermanshah for refusing to fire on protesters.
The Iranian regime deployed 800-5,000 Iranian-backed Iraqi militias for internal repression.
The Iranian regime labeled protesters 'terrorists' as part of its crackdown response.
Trump described Iranian leaders as infighting 'like cats' between moderates and hardliners.
Israel's defense minister stated readiness to renew war pending US approval in context of the extended Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.
Trump stated he faces no rush for a deal with Iran, emphasizing ample time while projecting confidence in munitions stocks.

regime_fall.current_status: Ceasefire expired April 22 with direct US-Iran military escalation intensifying: Trump ordered shoot-on-sight rules for Iranian vessels, US seized tankers and turned back 30+ ships, while IRGC attacked commercial shipping and seized vessels in retaliation. Tehran's fractured leadership—Ghalibaf's resignation, internal hardliner-diplomat rifts, and potential Provisional Leadership Council—faces simultaneous economic collapse (10% GDP loss from blockade), internal unrest requiring foreign militia deployment, and no clear decision-making authority as Araghchi positions for diplomatic engagement at Islamabad talks.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On April 24, 2026, President Trump ordered US forces to 'shoot and kill' Iranian vessels laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz and enforce a naval blockade with tanker seizures, marking direct kill-order escalation as the ceasefire expires and Pentagon released video of a second Iranian tanker seizure.
